MOTION - Petition To Appoint Neutral Arbitrator
Off Calendar Dec-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Braden, Esq. 415-595-4385 for Plaintiff.
Geoffrey Mires, Esq. of Rankin Shuey Ranucci Mintz Lampasana & Reynolds for Defendant ROBERT RABKIN et al.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ROBERT RABKIN, JOHN RABKIN, DAVID GREENBAUM, PACIFIC LAPAROSCOPY's Petition To Appoint Neutral Arbitrator.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants' unopposed petition to appoint neutral arbitrator is granted. The parties have reached an impasse in selecting a neutral arbitrator, in that the appointed party arbitrators have been unable to agree upon a neutral arbitrator. Accordingly, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.6, the Court nominates the following five persons from the list submitted by Defendants (Plaintiff has not submitted a list): Hon. George Hernandez (Ret.); Hon. Laurence Kay (Ret.); Hon. Raul Ramirez (Ret.); Hon. James Trembath (Ret.); Hon. W. Scott Snowden (Ret.). If the parties fail to select one of the listed arbitrators within five days of receipt of notice from the court, the court will appoint the neutral arbitrator from the nominees.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
